symptoms.symptom_name: Sharply demarcated chalk-white (fully depigmented) macule at the oral commissure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Full depigmentation (chalk-white), as opposed to partial hypopigmentation, is characteristic of vitiligo.
Pityriasis versicolor typically causes faintly hypopigmented, finely scaly patches rather than stark depigmentation.
Tissue necrosis and ischaemia are characteristically painful; painlessness argues strongly against them.
Vitiligo is asymptomatic — no pain or itch.

symptoms.symptom_name: Second matching depigmented macule on a fingertip (acrofacial distribution)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Simultaneous periorificial-facial and distal-digit involvement is the classic acrofacial vitiligo pattern.
Chemical leukoderma follows the pattern of contact, not a symmetric autoimmune acrofacial distribution.

symptoms.symptom_name: No scale, crust, blistering, ulceration or bleeding (intact skin surface)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fine scale reduces (but on video cannot fully exclude) a malassezia infection.
Intact, unbroken skin excludes ulceration and tissue death.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chemical, bleach or industrial exposure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chemical leukoderma requires exposure to phenolic/bleaching agents; a negative history makes it very unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Peri-lesional erythema following vigorous self-scrubbing (reactive, not inflammatory)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reactive erythema is an artefact of mechanical trauma and does not indicate infection or inflammation of the lesion itself; it is a red herring.
Absence of fever and systemic upset argues against invasive infection or sepsis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marked acute health anxiety with catastrophic misinterpretation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Disproportionate fear of a benign finding, mirror-checking and an urge to attend ED define the secondary health-anxiety component.
Under Wood's lamp, vitiliginous depigmentation shows bright blue-white accentuation — an in-person confirmatory test not available via telehealth.
Direct microscopy of a scraping definitively excludes malassezia; only obtainable in person.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Chalk-white, painless, non-scaly macules in a periorificial-plus-fingertip distribution with no chemical exposure together make benign vitiligo the overwhelmingly likely diagnosis and exclude the feared serious mimics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person dermatological examination (with Wood's lamp) at routine review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms depigmentation and distribution and excludes a superficial fungal mimic that cannot be judged on video — appropriately routine, not urgent.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Thyroid function tests (TSH) at routine GP review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Vitiligo is associated with autoimmune thyroid disease; routine screening is reasonable but not urgent.
There is no acute infective, necrotic or systemic process; urgent investigation would be over-investigation driven by the patient's panic.
